caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The frozen mix is four-digit 5415 rather than 541513, and its software-developer line is not a clean proxy for facilities operations.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Do not treat provider product launches as a direct survey of task automation at lower-middle-market firms.
inputs.pi_dist: ISG evidence is global managed services, not a lower-middle-market 541513 contract sample.
inputs.pi_dist: The selection concerns allocation of savings, not the amount of automatable work.
inputs.pi_moat: A regulated or sovereign specialist can be materially stronger than this broad-code estimate.
inputs.pi_moat: Human accountability supports residual demand but is not counted as automatic pricing power.
inputs.t50_years: No source reports 541513 lower-middle-market effective-adoption penetration or a direct t50 estimate.
inputs.t50_years: This is separate from cloud migration, which changes the addressable base rather than the rate of AI adoption.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Product availability and large-provider deployment are not equivalent to realized adoption at $1–10M EBITDA operators.
inputs.historical_analogs: The sources describe current market structure rather than a longitudinal, code-specific history.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: This is not a measured 541513 owner-age statistic and should not be used to infer seller supply.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence of evidence is not evidence of no shortage; it only prevents a positive score adjustment.
inputs.active_consolidators: Broad IT-services, MSP and provider-owned hosting deals are excluded because they cross the stated industry boundary.
inputs.buy_mult: Not an observed 541513 transaction multiple; hosting and MSP comparables were intentionally not used.
inputs.exit_mult: Not an observed 541513 transaction multiple; a differentiated regulated niche could exit differently.
inputs.pricing_structure: No survey of 541513 contract-form revenue weights was located; this must not be read as a measured mix.

The MELTING terminal thesis is supported principally by cloud substitution; references to AI reducing labor intensity overlap with the automation factor and should not be treated as independent terminal-demand evidence.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $266,793 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541513); class <5: 3,040 firms, avg revenue/firm $354,248 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $55,440 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 692 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,447,064 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $226,465 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 484 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,262,322 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $510,553 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 429 firms, avg revenue/firm $8,791,140 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,375,813 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 154 firms, avg revenue/firm $37,324,773 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $5,841,327 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 151 firms, avg revenue/firm $116,924,159 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $18,298,631 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 583

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: AIOps and autonomous service-management evidence supports substantial automation of monitoring, service desk and runbook work, while physical operations, accountability and client governance bound the task-level estimates.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541513 ($11,535,095,371) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541513 ($30,832,208,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Publishable with caveats. Exact mechanics, identity, digests, deterministic finalization, memo rendering, and arithmetic all pass. The four live sources support the broad automation, pricing, consolidation, and market-direction thesis; the record remains transparently low-confidence and preserves all authored scope and estimate caveats. The only additional publication caveat is a non-material overlap between AI task compression and terminal-demand prose.

sources.title: Global Tech Services Market Grows at Fastest Pace Ever in Q2, Propelled by Soaring AI Demand: ISG Index
sources.what_it_supports: 2026 managed-services growth, ITO direction, the much faster cloud XaaS/IaaS market, labour-intensive work displaced by LLMs, pricing deflation and provider-funded AI transformation in contracts.

sources.title: Index Insider | Scope, Shape and Strategy: AI's Impact on Deal Structures in 2026
sources.what_it_supports: Managed-services consolidation, client TCO objectives, providers pricing expected AI savings into deals, cost smoothing, and the pressure on undifferentiated mid-spend providers.

sources.title: Kyndryl reports fourth quarter and full-year 2026 results
sources.what_it_supports: A current large managed-infrastructure provider's Advanced Delivery AI automation and launch of Agentic Service Management for autonomous IT operations.

sources.title: Index Insider | 1H26 ISG Index Recap
sources.what_it_supports: First-half 2026 contrast between muted managed-services growth and strong IaaS growth, the negative AI expectation for labour-centric managed services, and the market's larger-provider consolidation pattern.

title: Computer Facilities Management Services
